name: Bristol Isambard 3
oneLiner: A Grace CPU-based Bristol supercomputer that complements Isambard-AI with energy-efficient national-scale HPC.
summary: Isambard 3 is a CPU-based supercomputer in Bristol that uses NVIDIA Grace CPU Superchips and is co-located with Isambard-AI. Public reporting describes it as delivering more than 55,000 cores and around six times the performance of Isambard 2. While it is not positioned as a flagship AI inference machine, it strengthens the UK's backbone compute capacity for data preparation, simulation, and CPU-heavy parts of AI workflows.

keyFacts.hardware: NVIDIA Grace CPU Superchips with more than 55,000 CPU cores
keyFacts.funding: GW4 and University of Bristol-backed research infrastructure
keyFacts.energy: Designed as an energy-efficient successor in the Isambard line

access.route: Access is offered through Bristol and partner research allocations, complementing the AIRR's Bristol footprint.

sources.label: University of Bristol - Isambard 3 goes online
